name: Wcla
description_short: WCLA Credit Union is a member-owned credit union in Olympia, WA specializing in serving timber industry workers and related professionals underserved by traditional banks.
description_long:
WCLA Credit Union operates as a not-for-profit, member-owned financial institution based in Olympia, Washington. The organization was founded with a specific mission to serve individuals in the timber industry and related fields—demographics traditionally underserved by conventional banks and larger credit unions. The credit union has built its reputation on personalized service and understanding the unique financial needs of its niche membership base.

The institution offers core credit union services including loan products, share accounts, and ATM access. Based on available information, WCLA provides personal loans and has extended credit to members as young as 18 years old. The organization maintains standard credit union hours (Monday-Friday, 8AM-5PM) and operates a physical branch location in central Olympia. As a federally insured credit union, member deposits are protected by NCUA insurance.

WCLA distinguishes itself through its specialized focus on an underserved market segment. Customer reviews specifically highlight the credit union's willingness to approve loans for timber industry workers when "traditional banks and credit unions fail to do so." The organization has cultivated a reputation for relationship-based banking, with reviews naming specific staff members (Tessa and Penny) and praising both employee expertise and customer service quality. This personalized approach appears to be central to the member experience.

A critical caveat exists: the Loc8NearMe website indicates the business may be permanently or temporarily closed, though this conflicts with the posted operating hours and recent customer reviews from 2020. Prospective members should independently verify current operational status before visiting or attempting to join. The limited public information available suggests WCLA maintains a small, specialized membership base rather than serving the general public, which may restrict accessibility for those outside the timber industry sector.
pros: Specializes in serving timber industry workers and related professionals typically rejected by mainstream banks
Willing to approve loans for applicants underserved by traditional financial institutions
Personalized service with named staff members acknowledged for expertise and customer care
Offers first-time loans to young applicants (members as young as 18)
Member-owned, not-for-profit structure returns profits to members
ATM access available at branch location
Maintains consistent weekday business hours (8AM-5PM Monday-Friday)
cons: Operational status unclear—website indicates possible permanent or temporary closure despite posted hours
Limited to niche membership (timber industry focus) may restrict eligibility for general public
Minimal public information about loan products, rates, terms, or membership requirements
Small organization may have limited branch network and service options compared to larger credit unions
No website provided; business information accessible only through third-party directory
best_for: Timber industry workers and forestry-related professionals seeking personal loans
Self-employed or contract workers in timber and related industries rejected by traditional banks
Young first-time borrowers in the timber industry seeking relationship-based lending
Members of underserved occupational groups seeking community-focused credit union services
diagnosis: WCLA Credit Union is best for timber industry workers and related professionals struggling to access credit through mainstream banks and larger credit unions. However, prospective members must first verify current operational status, as conflicting information suggests the branch may be closed; direct contact via phone (360-352-5033) is essential before attempting to apply or visit.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: Wcla — Credit Union in Olympia, WA | CreditDoc
meta_description: Wcla — NCUA-insured credit union in Olympia, WA. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
